Model_Name: Nyman2011 - Hierarchical insulin signaling and glucose homeostasis
Paper_Title: A hierarchical whole-body modeling approach elucidates the link between in vitro insulin signaling and in vivo glucose homeostasis
Model_Type: Mathematical Modelling Ontology: ordinary differential equation model
System_Modeled: Insulin signaling linked to adipose glucose uptake and whole-body glucose homeostasis
Primary_HRA_Linkage_Rationale_(LLM_infer: Best HRA anchor is adipose tissue;adipocyte, because the model explicitly bridges adipocyte insulin signaling to systemic glucose handling.
Mapping_Notes: Whole-body physiology is broader than a single tissue ontology mapping;adipose is the clearest direct atlas link.

Model_Name: Palmer2014 - IL-1beta-blocking therapies in T2DM (disease state)
Paper_Title: Effects of IL-1β-Blocking Therapies in Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus: A Quantitative Systems Pharmacology Modeling Approach to Explore Underlying Mechanisms
Model_Type: Mathematical Modelling Ontology: ordinary differential equation model
System_Modeled: Type 2 diabetes disease-state model of beta-cell dysfunction, inflammation, insulin secretion, and glucose-insulin dynamics
Primary_HRA_Linkage_Rationale_(LLM_infer: Strong atlas link through pancreas and pancreatic beta cell identity, plus inflammatory signaling relevant to diabetic cell states.
Mapping_Notes: Disease-state interpretation is central;beta-cell impairment and chronic IL-1beta effects are the primary atlas-facing concepts.

Model_Name: Palmer2014 - IL-1beta-blocking therapies in T2DM (healthy state)
Paper_Title: Effects of IL-1β-Blocking Therapies in Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus: A Quantitative Systems Pharmacology Modeling Approach to Explore Underlying Mechanisms
Model_Type: Mathematical Modelling Ontology: ordinary differential equation model
System_Modeled: Healthy-state comparator for beta-cell function, insulin secretion, and glucose-insulin dynamics
Primary_HRA_Linkage_Rationale_(LLM_infer: Provides a healthy pancreas and pancreatic beta cell reference state for comparison against the disease model using the same tissue and cell anchors.
Mapping_Notes: Same primary anatomy/cell mapping as disease state;main difference is parameterized physiological state rather than ontology target.

Paper_Title: A rule-based model of insulin signalling pathway
Model_Type: Mathematical Modelling Ontology: ordinary differential equation model
System_Modeled: Insulin receptor autophosphorylation, IRS recruitment, PI3K/Akt activation, and downstream insulin signaling logic
Primary_HRA_Linkage_Rationale_(LLM_infer: Best atlas anchor is insulin-responsive cell signaling machinery centered on insulin receptor pathway components rather than a specific organ.
Mapping_Notes: Tissue/cell mapping is inferred because the deposited model is pathway-centric rather than tissue-specific.

Paper_Title: The Akt switch model: Is location sufficient?
Model_Type: Mathematical Modelling Ontology: ordinary differential equation model
System_Modeled: Akt activation state and subcellular localization with implications for GLUT4-mediated glucose uptake
Primary_HRA_Linkage_Rationale_(LLM_infer: Primary HRA linkage is through cell-level Akt signaling and GLUT4-related glucose-response machinery in insulin-sensitive cells.
Mapping_Notes: Anatomy/cell assignment is inferred from the paper's insulin/GLUT4 context rather than an explicit tissue model.

Paper_Title: Quantifying the Contribution of the Liver to Glucose Homeostasis: A Detailed Kinetic Model of Human Hepatic Glucose Metabolism
Model_Type: Mathematical Modelling Ontology: ordinary differential equation model
System_Modeled: Hepatic glycolysis, gluconeogenesis, glycogen synthesis/breakdown, and hormonal control in T2DM
Primary_HRA_Linkage_Rationale_(LLM_infer: Direct HRA link is the liver as organ-level anchor, with hepatocyte metabolism providing the main mechanistic mapping target.
Mapping_Notes: Hormonal regulation is central, but the strongest atlas alignment is the hepatic compartment itself.

Model_Name: Huang2014 - IRS1 and IRS2 insulin signaling network
Paper_Title: Systematic modeling for the insulin signaling network mediated by IRS1 and IRS2
Model_Type: Mathematical Modelling Ontology: ordinary differential equation model
System_Modeled: Hepatic insulin signaling specificity through IRS1 and IRS2 network wiring and kinetics
Primary_HRA_Linkage_Rationale_(LLM_infer: Best atlas connection is cell-level insulin receptor substrate signaling, especially isoform-specific pathway logic within insulin-responsive cells.
Mapping_Notes: Paper explicitly frames the model as hepatic insulin signaling, so liver/hepatocyte mapping is direct rather than inferred.

Paper_Title: A pathway model of glucose-stimulated insulin secretion in the pancreatic beta-cell
Model_Type: Mathematical Modelling Ontology: ordinary differential equation model
System_Modeled: Pancreatic beta-cell glucose sensing and glucose-stimulated insulin secretion (GSIS)
Primary_HRA_Linkage_Rationale_(LLM_infer: Closest HRA anchor is the pancreatic beta cell because the model centers on glucose sensing and insulin secretion dynamics at that level.
Mapping_Notes: This is the most explicit beta-cell pathway model in the set;useful for linking GSIS components to atlas cell-state markers.
